differences.title: 1. DATA TYPES
differences.leftDesc: Supports advanced types like JSONB, arrays, geometric, and custom types.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily uses standard SQL data types, well-established and predictable.

differences.title: 2. EXTENSIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Allows custom functions, operators, data types, and indexes.
differences.rightDesc: Supports stored procedures, functions, and triggers.

differences.title: 3. ACID COMPLIANCE
differences.leftDesc: Ensures reliable transaction processing with Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability.
differences.rightDesc: Guarantees data integrity through ACID properties, widely trusted.

differences.title: 4. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Excels in complex queries, joins, and analytical workloads.
differences.rightDesc: Optimized for high-volume read operations and simpler queries.

differences.title: 5. JSON SUPPORT
differences.leftDesc: Efficient binary JSON format (JSONB) with indexing and operators.
differences.rightDesc: Supports JSON data type with functions for manipulation.

differences.title: 6. COMMUNITY & ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Active development, extensive documentation, and broad third-party support.
differences.rightDesc: Vast user base, wide tooling compatibility, and abundant resources.

differences.title: 7. INDEXING
differences.leftDesc: Supports B-tree, Hash, GiST, SP-GiST, GIN, and BRIN indexes.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily uses B-tree and Hash indexes.
